Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: Custom instruction:
Based on the notes, prepare one or two points each for the Method, Findings, Conclusions and Recommendations parts of the summary report. Write 100–175 words. Response: Below are the results of the surveys you requested, which were conducted in Rio de Janeiro and Shanghai.

To summarize our method: we took a sample covering all the demographics and asked how they felt about the collarless dress shirt

James Tufnell

Head of Research

Collarless dress shirt: facts and figures

Interviewed:

Rio de Janeiro – 1000

Shanghai – 1000

Rio de Janeiro:

Approve: 35% (would buy: 20%)                  Not approve: 65%

Shanghai:

Approve: 80% (would buy: 40%)                   Not approve: 20%

For the 65% in Rio and the 20% in Shanghai, it is not a highly desirable item for several reasons:

design

size

color

material

For the 20% in Rio and the 40% in Shanghai who 'would buy,' they expressed moderate satisfaction. Some factors that kept them from feeling strongly satisfied:

Before launching, not enough market research carried out

Good idea, bad execution

General feeling is that diversity was not considered in product design

Moving forward, this is what we should consider:

More research: what men want and need in an accessory

Design: more attention to detail, e.g. stitching color

Size: slim fitting

Colors: strong primary colors; black and white

Material: wrinkle-free and/or sweat absorbent

Changes for Rio are a priority, because Asia results are satisfactory

Some factors that kept them from feeling strongly satisfied: Before launching, not enough market research carried out Good idea, bad executio General feeling is that diversity was not considered in product desig Moving forward, this is what we should consider: More research: what men want and need in an accessory Design: more attention to detail, e.g. stitching color Size: slimfitting Colors: strong primary colors; black and white Material: wrinkle-free and/or sweatabsorbent Changes for Rio are a priority, because Asia results are satisfactory

Prompt Level: 14.0 Prompt: Your boss has asked you to write a formal report recommending a training company. You've chosen Skills Pro. Use your notes to write up the report. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: To: [Boss's Name]
From: [Your Name]
Date: [Current Date]
Subject: Recommendation for Training Provider

After evaluating two training companies, I recommend Skills Pro as our preferred training provider. 

While TrainQuick offers a cost-effective and flexible option, it has not gained traction in our other branch. Feedback indicates that employees prefer to avoid mixed classes with colleagues from different branches, which diminishes the overall effectiveness of their training.

In contrast, Skills Pro, although more expensive, delivers high-quality training. Participants have reported that while the course content is challenging, those who enroll often complete the courses successfully. Furthermore, Skills Pro is planning to expand its offerings, which could provide additional value for our employees in the future.

Investing in Skills Pro will ensure that our team receives comprehensive training and develops the necessary skills to excel in their roles. Quality is paramount, and Skills Pro’s reputation for effective course delivery supports our commitment to employee development. 

I recommend proceeding with Skills Pro for our training needs. 

Best regards,
[Your Name]
[Your Position]

Prompt Level: 15.0 Prompt: You're going to write about an interesting, fictional event that happened to a friend. Write your blog post. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: The Night My Friend Became a Ghost Hunter (Accidentally)

A few weeks ago, my friend Jane experienced something so bizarre, even she couldn’t believe it happened. It all started as a simple weekend getaway to a remote cabin in the woods, just the two of us, hoping for a peaceful escape. But, of course, nothing ever goes according to plan.

On the second night, Jane decided to take a walk around the property, while I stayed inside with a book. She returned an hour later, pale as a sheet. Apparently, she had found an old, abandoned graveyard hidden behind the trees. It wasn’t on any of the maps, and there was something unnervingly off about the place—an eerie stillness, like the air itself was holding its breath.

But here’s the kicker: As Jane walked away from the graveyard, she swore she saw a figure standing at the edge of the trees—faint, but undeniably there. She rushed back to the cabin and refused to go near that spot again. Ever since, she’s convinced she’s a magnet for ghosts, and now, every time we go on a trip, she packs a "ghost-hunting kit."

It seems like Jane's accidental brush with the paranormal turned her into a full-blown enthusiast—if only she'd known what she was getting into!
text_b: The Night My Friend Became a Ghost Hunter (Accidentally) A few weeks ago, my friend Jane experienced something so bizarre, even she couldn’t believe it happened. It all started as a simple weekend getaway to a remote cabin in the woods, just the two of us, hoping for a peaceful escape. But, of course, nothing ever goes according to plan. On the second night, Jane decided to take a walk around the property, while I stayed inside with a book. She returned an hour later, pale as a sheet. Apparently, she had found an old, abandoned graveyard hidden behind the trees. It wasn’t on any of the maps, and there was something unnervingly off about the place—an eerie stillness, like the air itself was holding its breath. But here’s the kicker: As Jane walked away from the graveyard, she swore she saw a figure standing at the edge of the trees—faint, but undeniably there. She rushed back to the cabin and refused to go near that spot again. Ever since, she’s convinced she’s a magnet for ghosts, and now, every time we go on a trip, she packs a "ghost-hunting kit." It seems like Jane's accidental brush with the paranormal turned her into a full-blown enthusiast—if only she'd known what she was getting into!

Prompt Level: 12.0 Prompt: Write about how to develop leadership qualities. You can write about personal experiences or what you have seen happen in others. What qualities do you think are most important? Do you think everyone has the potential to be a leader? Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Use your own words where possible. Response: Developing leadership qualities is an ongoing process that involves self-knowledge, practice and learning from experiences. Throughout my journey, I have observed and experienced some strategies that can be very effective.

One of the first lessons I learned was the importance of empathy. In a group project in college, I realized that listening to each team member's concerns and ideas not only strengthened our relationship but also led to more creative solutions. When a leader puts himself in others' shoes, he creates an environment of trust and collaboration, essential for the group's success.

Most important quality is emphaty because empathy is an extraordinary quality that brings countless benefits to both personal and professional relationships. When we are empathetic, we are able to put ourselves in someone else's shoes, understanding their emotions, challenges and perspectives. This not only strengthens bonds between people, but also creates an environment of trust and respect.

Not everyone can be a leader, you are responsible for every problem and need to be the center of a reunion

Prompt Level: 11.0 Prompt: Write a paragraph about how media has changed in your lifetime. What media was important before? What do you use now, and how has it changed your reading habits? Pay attention to your use of commas, colons and semicolons. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Use your own words where possible. Response: Media has undergone a significant transformation over the years. Previously, newspapers and television were the primary sources of information; people relied on them for news, entertainment, and educational content. However, with the advent of the internet and smartphones, digital media has taken center stage. Today, social media platforms, online news websites, and streaming services are the go-to sources for information and entertainment. This shift has changed my reading habits considerably: I now consume news in real-time through apps and websites, rather than waiting for the morning paper. Additionally, I follow various blogs and social media accounts to stay updated on topics of interest. The convenience of accessing information anytime, anywhere has made reading more frequent and diverse, but it also requires discernment to filter through the vast amount of content available.

Moreover, the interactive nature of digital media allows for immediate feedback and engagement, which was not possible with traditional media. I can now participate in discussions, share my opinions, and connect with others who have similar interests. This has made the consumption of media a more dynamic and engaging experience. However, it also means that I need to be more critical of the sources I trust, as misinformation can spread quickly online. Overall, the evolution of media has made information more accessible and interactive, but it also demands a higher level of media literacy.

I can now participate in discussions, share my opinions, and connect with others who have similar interests. This has made the consumption of media a more dynamic and engaging experience. However, it also means that I need to be more critical of the sources I trust, as misinformation can spread quickly online. Overall, the evolution of media has made information more accessible and interactive, but it also demands a higher level of media literacy.

Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: Rewrite the termination section of the contract to take into account possible discrimination either against or by the employer or employee. Compare your amendment with the model. This is a non-graded activity. Response: 7. Termination of Employment
7.1 Subsequent to completion of the probationary period, this employment may be terminated by either Party with or without cause.
7.2 Termination without cause:
 * By Party A (Employer): Party A may terminate the employment of Party B without cause by providing Party B with advance notice of termination or compensation in lieu of notice as specified in Section 7.2.1.
 * By Party B (Employee): Party B may terminate his employment at any time by providing Party A with at least eight (8) weeks written notice of his intention to resign.
7.2.1 Notice or Compensation in Lieu of Notice: The notice period or compensation in lieu of notice for termination without cause by Party A shall be as follows:
 * One (1) month for employees with less than one (1) year of continuous service.
 * One (1) month plus two (2) weeks for each additional year of continuous service up to a maximum of six (6) months.
7.3 Termination with cause:
 * By Party A (Employer): Party A may terminate the employment of Party B with cause for any of the following reasons:
   * Gross misconduct, including but not limited to:
     * Theft, fraud, or dishonesty.
     * Serious or repeated violations of company policies.
     * Use of illegal drugs or alcohol on company premises.
     * Engaging in conduct that creates a hostile or unsafe work environment.
     * Discrimination or harassment based on any protected characteristic (e.g., race, gender, religion, sexual orientation, disability).
   * Willful disobedience of lawful and reasonable instructions.
   * Incompetence or failure to meet performance standards after receiving adequate training and support.
   * Unauthorized absence from work.

text_a:
Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: Custom instruction:
Based on the notes, prepare one or two points each for the Method, Findings, Conclusions and Recommendations parts of the summary report. Write 100–175 words. Response: Report on Collarless Dress Shirt Survey Results

Dear Mr. Samson,
I am pleased to present the results of the surveys conducted in Rio de Janeiro and Shanghai regarding the collarless dress shirt. The survey aimed to gauge consumer opinions across various demographics in both cities.
Survey Methodology:
A sample of 1000 individuals was interviewed in each city. The primary objective was to assess the public’s reception of the collarless dress shirt, with a focus on their approval, purchase intent, and specific preferences or concerns.
Results:
Rio de Janeiro (1000 Participants):
•	Approval Rate: 35% 
o	Would Buy: 20%
•	Disapproval Rate: 65%
Shanghai (1000 Participants):
•	Approval Rate: 80% 
o	Would Buy: 40%
•	Disapproval Rate: 20%
For the 65% of participants in Rio who did not approve, the key reasons were:
•	Design Issues
•	Size Concerns
•	Color Preferences
•	Material Quality
For the 20% of participants in Shanghai who did not approve, the reasons were similar.

For the 20% in Rio and the 40% in Shangai who would purchase the collarless dress shirt, their satisfaction was moderate. The main points of dissatisfaction included:
•	Insufficient Market Research
•	Good Idea, Poor Execution
•	Lack of Diversity in Design

Recommendations:
Based on the survey feedback, we recommend the following actions to improve the collarless dress shirt's appeal:
1.	Increased Research: More comprehensive market research on men's needs in an accessory
2.	Improved Design: Focus on finer details, such as stitching color
3.	Size Options: Offer a slim fit to reach a more specific market segment.
4.	Color Palette: Opt for  primary colors like black and white.
5.	Material Improvements: Use wrinkle-free or sweat-absorbent fabrics to enhance comfort and usability.

The results in Rio de Janeiro show a significant gap in consumer approval, suggesting that changes to the product should be prioritized for the Brazilian market. On the other hand, Shanghai's results are more positive, but improvements could further increase satisfaction.

If you have any further questions or need additional information, please don’t hesitate to contact me.

Best regards,
James Tufnell
Head of Research

Results: Rio de Janeiro (1000 Participants): • Approval Rate: 35% o Would Buy: 20% • Disapproval Rate: 65% Shanghai (1000 Participants): • Approval Rate: 80% o Would Buy: 40% • Disapproval Rate: 20% For the 65% of participants in Rio who did not approve, the key reasons were: • Design Issues • Size Concerns • Color Preferences • Material Quality For the 20% of participants in Shanghai who did not approve, the reasons were similar. For the 20% in Rio and the 40% in Shangai who would purchase the collarless dress shirt, their satisfaction was moderate. Prompt Level: 16.0 Prompt: Write a description of a legendary creature that’s popular in your country, or one you find interesting. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: One of the most fascinating legendary creatures from Japanese folklore is the **Kitsune**. These mythical foxes are revered for their intelligence and magical abilities. Kitsune, which means "fox" in Japanese, are believed to possess the power to shapeshift into human form, often transforming into beautiful women to trick or enchant humans. They are associated with Inari, the Shinto deity of rice, fertility, and prosperity, and are thought to act as the deity's messengers.

Kitsune are known to have multiple tails, with legends stating that they can grow up to nine tails as they age and gain more wisdom and power. A nine-tailed fox is considered the most powerful and is known as a **Kyūbi no Kitsune**. These creatures are also said to have the ability to generate fire or lightning, possess incredible longevity, and have the capacity to enter and manipulate dreams.

While Kitsune can be benevolent and protective, they can also be mischievous or malevolent, depending on the tale. They often serve as moral lessons, warning against greed, arrogance, and disrespect towards others. The duality of the Kitsune—capable of both kindness and trickery—adds a rich complexity to their legend, making them one of the most enduring and intriguing figures in Japanese mythology.

Mr. Medina, who was reportedly lost and seeking shelter in an empty house, damaged the property and caused emotional distress to the family. His defense centered around confusion and the necessity to survive in an inhospitable environment. However, the judge ruled that trespassing, even under these circumstances, constitutes a criminal offense, as Mr. Medina had entered private property without consent.
The judge found Mr. Medina guilty of trespassing and awarded Mr. and Mrs. Byron $1,000 in damages, divided into $500 for basic damages and $500 for aggravated damages. Additionally, Mr. Medina was sentenced to 300 hours of community service. The judge emphasized that the trespasser had violated the Byron family’s trust and subjected them to unnecessary emotional distress.
The verdict seems appropriate, if a little severe on the human level, as Mr. Medina’s actions disrupted the family’s sense of security, regardless of his intentions. Trespassing is a serious offense, and the damages and community service requirement provide a balanced approach to justice

Response: "O Stree Repu Ra" is a popular phrase from Indian folklore, particularly in the southern states, translating to "Oh Lady, come tomorrow." This phrase is associated with a ghostly legend of a female spirit, often referred to as "Stree" or "Chudail," who is believed to roam at night, preying on men. According to the legend, she knocks on doors and calls out to potential victims. To protect themselves, people write "O Stree Repu Ra" outside their homes, hoping to deter her by suggesting she return the next day.

The legend inspired the 2018 Bollywood film "Stree," followed by stree 2 which humorously and critically explores societal themes like gender dynamics while drawing from this folklore. The film portrays the spirit as a misunderstood figure, adding depth to the traditional narrative. This tale, blending fear with cultural commentary, continues to captivate audiences, reflecting societal fears and the power of folklore in modern storytelling.

Prompt Level: 16.0 Prompt: In the preceding units, nine writing techniques were introduced: metaphor, hyperbole, symbolism, free writing, focused free writing, loop writing, listing, questioning and cubing. Write a paragraph or two explaining which techniques seem most useful to you. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: Cubing and loop writing are both creative techniques used in the fields of writing and thinking to organize and develop ideas.
Cubing is a method inspired by the idea of looking at an object from all six sides. In writing, it involves exploring a topic from six different perspectives to gain a more comprehensive understanding. These perspectives include describing, comparing, associating, analyzing, applying, and arguing. For example, when writing about a historical event, a writer might first describe the event, then compare it to similar events, analyze its causes and effects, and argue its significance. Cubing helps expand ideas and allows for a more multidimensional approach to a topic, fostering deeper thinking and more nuanced writing.
Loop writing is a technique in which a writer continually refines and revises their work through repeated drafts. In loop writing, a writer begins with a basic idea or rough draft and then revises it in loops, each time deepening the focus or expanding on specific details. The process can help clarify ideas, improve structure, and strengthen arguments. It's particularly effective for exploring new concepts, allowing writers to see connections between thoughts that may not have been apparent in the initial draft.
Both cubing and loop writing encourage flexible, iterative thinking, offering structured ways to enhance creativity and thoughtfulness in writing.
text_b: Cubing and loop writing are both creative techniques used in the fields of writing and thinking to organize and develop ideas. Cubing is a method inspired by the idea of looking at an object from all six sides. In writing, it involves exploring a topic from six different perspectives to gain a more comprehensive understanding. These perspectives include describing, comparing, associating, analyzing, applying, and arguing. For example, when writing about a historical event, a writer might first describe the event, then compare it to similar events, analyze its causes and effects, and argue its significance. Cubing helps expand ideas and allows for a more multidimensional approach to a topic, fostering deeper thinking and more nuanced writing. Loop writing is a technique in which a writer continually refines and revises their work through repeated drafts. In loop writing, a writer begins with a basic idea or rough draft and then revises it in loops, each time deepening the focus or expanding on specific details. The process can help clarify ideas, improve structure, and strengthen arguments. It's particularly effective for exploring new concepts, allowing writers to see connections between thoughts that may not have been apparent in the initial draft. Both cubing and loop writing encourage flexible, iterative thinking, offering structured ways to enhance creativity and thoughtfulness in writing.

• Warehouse
•Employees
• customers’ file

Buyer agrees to accept the goods and pay for them according to the following terms:
• Half payment due upon receipt of goods, with full payment due within 30 days of delivery.

Buyer has the right to examine the goods and has 21 days to notify seller of any claim for damages. Failure to provide such notice constitutes acceptance of the goods.

Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: You're a foreman on a construction site. You're about to meet with your new workers for the first time and need to go through the safety rules with them. Make notes below regarding the rules you need to tell them. Response: ✅ Safety Gear

Hard hats, safety boots, gloves, and reflective vests must be worn at all times.

Use eye and ear protection when using power tools.

✅ Site Conduct

No smoking or alcohol on site.

Stay alert and avoid horseplay.

Report any unsafe conditions immediately.

✅ Equipment Use

Only authorized personnel may operate machinery.

Check tools and equipment before use.

✅ Ladders and Scaffolding

Ensure ladders are stable before climbing.

Use harnesses on scaffolding where required.

✅ Emergency Procedures

Know the location of first aid kits and fire extinguishers.

Report all accidents, even minor ones, immediately.

Follow evacuation procedures in case of fire or other emergencies.

✅ Clean Work Area

Keep pathways clear of debris.

Store tools and materials safely when not in use.

Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: Custom instruction:
Based on the notes, prepare one or two points each for the Method, Findings, Conclusions and Recommendations parts of the summary report. Write 100–175 words. Response: Report: the results of the surveys about Collarless dress shirt: facts and figures

Introduction: the surveys were requested in Rio de Janeiro and Shanghai.

Method: Interviewed: Rio de Janeiro – 1000, Shanghai – 1000 people. Survey's question was  how they felt about the collarless dress shirt.

Findings: People living in Rio de Janeiro approved: 35% (would buy: 20%), not approved: 65%; in Shanghai approved: 80% (would buy: 40%), not approved: 20%

Conclusions: For the 65% in Rio and the 20% in Shanghai, it is not a highly desirable item for several reasons:
design, size, color, material.

General feeling is that diversity was not considered in product design.

Recommendation: 
We recommend to make more research: what men want and need in an accessory.

There should be more focus on design: more attention to detail, e.g. stitching color, size: slim fitting, colors: strong primary colors; black and white.

It is important to change  a priority in Rio, since in  Asia results are satisfactory.

Method: Interviewed: Rio de Janeiro – 1000, Shanghai – 1000 people. Survey's question was how they felt about the collarless dress shirt. Findings: People living in Rio de Janeiro approved: 35% (would buy: 20%), not approved: 65%; in Shanghai approved: 80% (would buy: 40%), not approved: 20% Conclusions: For the 65% in Rio and the 20% in Shanghai, it is not a highly desirable item for several reasons: design, size, color, material. General feeling is that diversity was not considered in product design. Recommendation: We recommend to make more research: what men want and need in an accessory. There should be more focus on design: more attention to detail, e.g. stitching color, size: slim fittin, colors: strong primary colors; blackand whit. It is important to change a priority in Rio, since in Asia results are satisfactory.

Prompt Level: 15.0 Prompt: Write a comparison-contrast essay about your favorite city and another place. Use the 'X then Y' structure. Be sure to polish your work before you submit it. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: Visakhapatnam, also known as Vizag, is a lively city by the sea in Andhra Pradesh, India. It has beautiful beaches, green hills, and a mix of modern and traditional culture. Some popular places in Vizag are Ramakrishna Beach, Kailasagiri Hill Park, and the INS Kursura Submarine Museum. The city has good facilities like shopping malls, schools, and hospitals, making it a great place to live and visit. Vizag's mix of city life and cultural heritage attracts many people.

On the other hand, my hometown Narsipatnam is a small town in the Eastern Ghats of Andhra Pradesh. Unlike the busy streets of Vizag, Narsipatnam is quiet and peaceful. It is surrounded by green hills and beautiful nature, perfect for those who love the outdoors. Life in Narsipatnam is slower, and people know each other well. The town doesn't have as many modern facilities as Vizag, but it has a simple and charming atmosphere. The local markets, traditional festivals, and friendly people make it feel like home.

While Visakhapatnam shines with its modern city life and scenic beaches, Narsipatnam offers a calm and natural beauty. Both places are special to me, each giving me different experiences that I cherish.
text_b: Visakhapatnam, also known as Vizag, is a lively city by the sea in Andhra Pradesh, India. It has beautiful beaches, green hills, and a mix of modern and traditional culture. Some popular places in Vizag are Ramakrishna Beach, Kailasagiri Hill Park, and the INS Kursura Submarine Museum. The city has good facilities like shopping malls, schools, and hospitals, making it a great place to live and visit. Vizag's mix of city life and cultural heritage attracts many people. On the other hand, my hometownNarsipatnamis a small town in the Eastern Ghats of Andhra Pradesh. Unlike the busy streets of Vizag, Narsipatnam is quiet and peaceful. It is surrounded by green hills and beautiful nature, perfect for those who love the outdoors. Life in Narsipatnam is slower, and people know each other well. The town doesn't have as many modern facilities as Vizag, but it has a simple and charming atmosphere. The local markets, traditional festivals, and friendly people make it feel like home. While Visakhapatnam shines with its modern city life and scenic beaches, Narsipatnam offers a calm and natural beauty. Both places are special to me, each giving me different experiences that I cherish.

Write an email to the boss describing two challenges and solutions. Type in the input box. Write 75-130 words. Use your own words where possible. Response: 
Subject: Future Challenges and Possible Solutions

Dear Mr. Silva,

I hope you’re doing well. I’m writing to share two concerns that may impact our company in the near future.
First, we’re experiencing low employee engagement, which may reduce productivity. I suggest organizing regular team-building activities and offering professional development workshops.
Second, we are seeing a decline in customer retention. To address this, we could improve our customer service by implementing a feedback system and offering loyalty programs.
I believe these steps can help us stay ahead of potential issues.
Best regards,
Rita de Cássia

text_a: Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: You work for an electronics company and are preparing a speech for a celebration dinner. Include a greeting/opening, a part where you show thanks, and a conclusion where you try to motivate the staff. Type in the input box. Write 125–200 words. Response: Good evening, ladies and gentlemen, and a warm welcome to all of you. We're here tonight to celebrate the sales achievement of this year. I'd like to begin with a quote from Thomas Edison, who said that there's no substitute for hard work. I think this quote articulates that there really are no short cuts in our industry, and success only comes with hard work. Our success is thanks to the dedication that our staff has brought to work every day. Your hard work is the reason we are here tonight. Thank you for your attention, everyone. In closing, I would like to quote from Winston Churchill, who said that success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts. Let's come together for next year.

text_a: Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: Your teacher has asked you to write a short text about the parts of an airplane. Explain what each of the different parts are for, including the fuselage, wings, tail and engines. Write 150-200 words. Response: A plane can be divided in 4, the fuselage, the wings, the tail and the engine. The fuselage accommodates cargo, passengers and crew members. It also has a specific shape that helps with aerodynamics and acts as the assembly base for the rest of the aircraft. The wings are in charge of keeping the plane flying by generating lift and control the airplanes roll by adjusting flaps. The tail stabilizes and steers the plane (side to side and up and down movement) and helps the aircraft remain stable and controlled. The engine is in charge of providing the sufficient thrust for take off and to propel the air forward while in the air. Without this movement wouldn't be possible. There are different types of engines like jet engines and piston engines which are suitable for different kind of airplanes. Each one of this parts works with the others and is mandatory for the plane to fly.
text_b: A plane can be divided in 4, the fuselage, the wings, the tailand the engine. The fuselage accommodates cargo, passengersand crew members. It also has a specific shape that helps with aerodynamics and acts as the assembly base for the rest of the aircraft. The wings are in charge of keeping the plane flying by generating lift and control the airplanes roll by adjusting flaps. The tail stabilizes and steers the plane (side to side and up and down movement) and helps the aircraft remain stable and controlled. The engine is in charge of providing the sufficient thrust for take off and to propel the air forward while in the air. Without this movement wouldn't be possible. There are different types of engineslike jet engines and piston engineswhich are suitable for different kind of airplanes. Each one of this parts works with the others and is mandatory for the plane to fly.

Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: As there were a few problems with Cessna 123's landing, you have been asked to write a report by your superiors. Write the report below. Write 100 - 150 words. Response: Dear Sully,
As you might already know I faced and abnormal situation in my last flight with aircraft Cessna 123. When approaching to land in San Jose airport, I noticed that the flaps were jammed, unnabled to extend. I aborted the landing and circled in order to perform the appropriate checklists to deal with that situattion. Unfortunatelly, the problem could not be solved. I then decided to divert to San Pablo to use their longer runway, as the land will need to be performed with  greater speed and it will require greater distance to stop the aircraft, as consequence. I was able to perform the maneuver appropriatelly and land safely, with no consequence to the aircraft or to our passangers, unless the need to then to take an one hour bus trip to reach their original destination, of course.

Response: Leadership is about more than titles; it’s about making a positive impact through actions and inspiring others. The best leaders I’ve seen are empathetic, humble, and willing to learn. For example, I once had a leader who didn’t hesitate to help with small tasks like flipping treatment rooms, showing that true leadership means teamwork and approachability.

The most important leadership qualities are communication, integrity, and adaptability. Great leaders listen actively, build trust through honesty, and motivate their teams toward shared goals. They’re not perfect but learn from mistakes and adapt to challenges with resilience.

I believe everyone has the potential to be a leader, but it takes effort to develop the necessary skills. Leadership starts with self-awareness, recognizing strengths and weaknesses, and being open to feedback. Whether leading a team or setting an example, leadership is about growing, helping others succeed, and creating a positive environment.

Response: Challenge 1: Resource Allocation for Project X

As you know, Project X has been progressing slower than anticipated due to limited resources. The current team is stretched thin, impacting both productivity and morale.

Solution: I propose we temporarily reassign two team members from Project Y, which is currently ahead of schedule, to Project X. This will help balance the workload and accelerate progress. Additionally, we should consider hiring a temporary contractor for specialized tasks, which will allow our team to focus on core activities.

Challenge 2: Communication Gaps Between Departments

We've noticed some miscommunication between the marketing and sales departments, leading to inconsistent messaging and missed opportunities.

Solution: Implementing bi-weekly inter-departmental meetings could enhance alignment and ensure everyone is on the same page. Furthermore, utilizing a shared digital platform for project updates and feedback can facilitate better collaboration and real-time communication.

I believe these solutions will help us navigate the current challenges and improve overall efficiency. Please let me know if you would like to discuss this further or if there's anything else you would recommend.

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Isaq Shariff

Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: Write a report about a collision between your vessel and a right whale. Use your own ideas. Write 100-150 words. Type in the input box. Response: Collision Report: Vessel and Right Whale

Date: 06 July 2025
Time: 03:45 UTC
Location: 25°30'N, 77°00'W (Northwest Atlantic Ocean)

At approximately 03:45 UTC, while navigating at reduced speed in compliance with marine mammal protection guidelines, our vessel, MV Ocean Star, unexpectedly collided with a North Atlantic right whale. The whale surfaced directly ahead, leaving the bridge team with no time to take evasive action.

Upon impact, the vessel was immediately brought to a full stop, and visual confirmation of the whale was attempted. The whale was seen swimming away slowly, though signs of injury were unclear. The incident was reported to the appropriate maritime and environmental authorities.

No damage to the vessel or injuries to the crew were reported. The crew has been reminded of procedures when navigating in known whale zones. A full investigation and review of navigational practices is underway.

I’ve got to tell you about something absolutely bizarre that happened to me last weekend. I decided to try a new yoga class at this trendy studio in the city. I thought it would be a relaxing way to spend my Saturday morning, but things didn’t exactly go as planned.

So, there I was, in the middle of a "relaxing" session when suddenly, the instructor, who was super serious, asked us to do a pose called "the crow." It's supposed to be one of those impressive poses where you balance your whole body on your hands. Now, I’m not the most graceful person, and trying it felt like a disaster waiting to happen. I got up, attempted the pose, and... well, I fell right onto the floor, straight onto my back. Everyone in the class just froze and then burst out laughing, including the instructor! It was a bit embarrassing, but honestly, it was hilarious in the end. I didn’t master "the crow," but I definitely got a good laugh out of it.

Hope your weekend was less eventful (but in a good way)!

Talk soon,
Jeevitha

Prompt Level: 15.0 Prompt: Apply the language you've learned in this lesson to a new topic: language learning. Write about issues and challenges that language students often face. Ask yourself guiding questions to help organize the writing. Type in the input box. Write 150-250 words. Response: Learning a new language is an adventure with hurdles! Students often have to struggle with university work, new vocabulary, rules of grammar and then their work. Mastering pronunciation can also be challenging especially when it is significantly different from their native language. Access to authentic learning resource and opportunity for real life interaction can also be limited which makes it harder to immerse in the language.
Another level of difficulty arises from the diverse learning styles among students. What works well for one person might nit be as effective as for another. Some thrive with visual aids, some through auditory inputs and so on. Prompt Level: 16.0 Prompt: Write a paragraph describing how robots are being used in your country. How do you feel about them? In what ways will they positively and negatively affect society? Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: In my country, robots are increasingly being used in various fields such as manufacturing, healthcare, agriculture, and even customer service. They help assemble products in factories, assist doctors during surgeries, and perform repetitive tasks like cleaning or delivering food in restaurants. I feel both excited and a bit concerned about their growing presence.
 On the positive side, robots can improve efficiency, reduce human error, and take over dangerous jobs, making life safer and more convenient. 
 I feel both curious and hopeful about robots. However, they might also lead to job losses and reduce human interaction, which could negatively affect social skills and employment. Overall, robots bring many benefits, but society must find a balance to use them wisely and fairly.
Recently, some stores and restaurants have started using robots to greet customers or deliver food. As technology continues to advance, the role of robots in daily life is expected to grow even more.

Prompt Level: 16.0 Prompt: In the preceding units, nine writing techniques were introduced: metaphor, hyperbole, symbolism, free writing, focused free writing, loop writing, listing, questioning and cubing. Write a paragraph or two explaining which techniques seem most useful to you. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: Among the nine writing techniques introduced, metaphor and symbolism stand out as particularly useful. Metaphors allow writers to convey complex ideas more vividly by making direct comparisons that spark imagination in readers. For instance, describing a challenging experience as "climbing a steep mountain" evokes not only the struggle but also a sense of achievement at the summit. This technique enhances emotional resonance in writing.

Symbolism, on the other hand, adds depth by allowing objects, characters, or events to represent larger ideas. A recurring symbol can unify a narrative and provide layers of meaning, inviting readers to engage more profoundly with the text.

Additionally, focused free writing serves as a valuable tool for overcoming writer's block. By allowing thoughts to flow freely without judgment, one can uncover hidden ideas or themes that may lead to more polished writing later on. Overall, these techniques provide powerful avenues for expression and creativity, enabling writers to connect with readers on a deeper level while effectively communicating their message.

A recurring symbol can unify a narrative and provide layers of meaning, inviting readers to engage more profoundly with the text. Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: Custom instruction:
Based on the notes, prepare one or two points each for the Method, Findings, Conclusions and Recommendations parts of the summary report. Write 100–175 words. Response: Method:

Surveys were conducted with a 1,000-person sample in both Rio de Janeiro and Shanghai, covering a broad demographic.

Respondents were asked about their opinions on the collarless dress shirt, focusing on approval, likelihood to purchase, and factors influencing their views.

Findings:

In Rio de Janeiro, 35% approved of the shirt (20% willing to purchase), while 65% disapproved.

In Shanghai, 80% approved (40% willing to purchase), with 20% disapproving.

Key reasons for disapproval in both cities included design, size, color, and material concerns.

Conclusions:

The collarless dress shirt was more positively received in Shanghai, but both cities showed moderate satisfaction from potential buyers.

There is significant room for improvement in the product’s execution, particularly regarding diversity and customer needs.

Recommendations:

Conduct more detailed market research to understand customer preferences in both regions.

Revise the design by focusing on detail (e.g., stitching and color), size (slim fit), and material (wrinkle-free, sweat-absorbent).

Prioritize changes for Rio, as its results were more unfavorable compared to Shanghai.
text_b: Method: Surveys were conducted with a 1,000-person sample in both Rio de Janeiro and Shanghai, covering a broad demographic. Respondents were asked about their opinions on the collarless dress shirt, focusing on approval, likelihood to purchase, and factors influencing their views. Findings: In Rio de Janeiro, 35% approved of the shirt (20% willing to purchase), while 65% disapproved. In Shanghai, 80% approved (40% willing to purchase), with 20% disapproving. Key reasons for disapproval in both cities included design, size, color, and material concerns. Conclusions: The collarless dress shirt was more positively received in Shanghai, but both cities showed moderate satisfaction from potential buyers. There is significant room for improvement in the product’s execution, particularly regarding diversity and customer needs. Recommendations: Conduct more detailed market research to understand customer preferences in both regions. Revise the design by focusing on detail (e.g., stitching and color), size (slim fit), and material (wrinkle-free, sweat-absorbent). Prioritize changes for Rio, as its results were more unfavorable compared to Shanghai.

Prompt Level: 13.0 Prompt: Use the information in the notes to write an informative text about NAFTA, or write about a trade agreement your country is a part of. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: The North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) is a trilateral trade bloc formed by the United States, Canada, and Mexico, aimed at reducing or eliminating customs duties and trade barriers among its member states. Implemented on January 1, 1994, NAFTA has significantly impacted trade relations, particularly in agriculture.

NAFTA led to the immediate elimination of tariffs on over half of Mexico's exports to the US and over a third of US exports to Mexico. The agreement phased out tariffs on US-Mexico trade within 10-15 years, fostering increased trade volumes. NAFTA boosted agricultural trade, with Mexico becoming a significant importer of US agricultural products. NAFTA benefited North American economies, increasing productivity and competitiveness.

Some industries, like manufacturing, experienced job losses due to increased competition. And NAFTA's investor-state dispute settlement provisions have raised concerns about environmental protection.

NAFTA was replaced by the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) on July 1, 2020, which modernizes trade relations and addresses new economic and environmental challenges.
text_b: The North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) is a trilateral trade bloc formed by the United States, Canada, and Mexico, aimed at reducing or eliminating customs duties and trade barriers among its member states. Implemented on January 1, 1994, NAFTA has significantly impacted trade relations, particularly in agriculture. NAFTA led to the immediate elimination of tariffs on over half of Mexico's exports to the US and over a third of US exports to Mexico. The agreement phased out tariffs on US-Mexico trade within 10-15 years, fostering increased trade volumes. NAFTA boosted agricultural trade, with Mexico becoming a significant importer of US agricultural products. NAFTA benefited North American economies, increasing productivity and competitiveness. Some industries, like manufacturing, experienced job losses due to increased competition. And NAFTA's investor-state dispute settlement provisions have raised concerns about environmental protection. NAFTA was replaced by the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) on July 1, 2020, which modernizes trade relations and addresses new economic and environmental challenges.

Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: What problems can happen on board a ship? What are some solutions? Use your own ideas. Choose from one or two of the options provided and write 80-100 words. Options: general cargo safety and stowage; staffing levels and working conditions; general condition and maintenance of the vessel; safety standards; pollution Response: One major issue on board a ship is staffing levels and working conditions. Understaffing can lead to crew fatigue, increasing the risk of accidents and inefficiency. Poor working conditions, such as long hours and lack of proper rest, can also affect morale and productivity.  
To address this, shipping companies should ensure adequate crew numbers and enforce proper rest periods. Implementing regular training and mental health support can further improve working conditions. Additionally, strict adherence to international labor standards can help protect seafarers' rights and overall well-being.

text_a: Prompt Level: 16.0 Prompt: In the preceding units, nine writing techniques were introduced: metaphor, hyperbole, symbolism, free writing, focused free writing, loop writing, listing, questioning and cubing. Write a paragraph or two explaining which techniques seem most useful to you. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: Among the nine writing techniques introduced, I find metaphor, focused free writing, and questioning to be the most useful for developing my ideas and enhancing my expression. Metaphor allows me to express abstract thoughts and emotions in creative and powerful ways by comparing them to more tangible or relatable things. This helps deepen meaning and create vivid images for the reader. Focused free writing is especially helpful when I have a topic in mind but don’t know where to begin. It helps me stay on track while still exploring new angles of the subject. It encourages flow without the pressure of perfection. Lastly, questioning helps me explore my topic in depth by challenging my assumptions and pushing me to consider different perspectives. Asking “why,” “how,” and “what if” opens up new directions in my writing and strengthens the quality of my arguments or narratives. These three techniques together make my writing more engaging, clear, and original.

Prompt Level: 16.0 Prompt: Write a paragraph describing how robots are being used in your country. How do you feel about them? In what ways will they positively and negatively affect society? Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: In my country, robots are increasingly integrated into various sectors, from manufacturing to healthcare. In factories, automated machines streamline production processes, enhancing efficiency and reducing costs. In hospitals, robotic systems assist in surgeries, allowing for greater precision and shorter recovery times. Additionally, robots are being deployed in agriculture to monitor crops and optimize resource use, contributing to more sustainable farming practices.

I feel cautiously optimistic about the role of robots in society. On the positive side, they can alleviate labor shortages, improve safety in hazardous environments, and enhance productivity across industries. However, there are significant concerns as well. The rise of automation could lead to job displacement, particularly for low-skilled workers, exacerbating economic inequalities. Furthermore, reliance on robots may lead to ethical dilemmas, especially in areas like decision-making in healthcare or military applications. Balancing the benefits and challenges of robotic technology will be crucial as we navigate this evolving landscape, ensuring that innovation serves the greater good while minimizing negative impacts on society.

Both car designs present compelling features. Design A excels in acceleration and top speed, making it ideal for performance-oriented drivers. Its sleek aerodynamic design contributes to its overall appeal. On the other hand, Design B offers a more balanced approach with its focus on handling and control. Its slightly larger wheelbase and tire configuration provide superior stability, making it a better choice for those who prioritize maneuverability and safety.

Recommendation:

Given our target market and the current trends in the automotive industry, I believe Design B is the most promising choice for next year's rollout. While Design A's raw power and speed are impressive, Design B's emphasis on handling and control offers a more well-rounded package that will appeal to a wider range of consumers. Its focus on safety and maneuverability aligns with the growing demand for vehicles that are both exciting to drive and practical for everyday use.

I strongly recommend adopting Design B for our next year's model. Its combination of performance, handling, and safety will position us as a leader in the automotive market.

Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: You are a journalist for a financial magazine. You've been asked by your editor to write a report on the performance of the Hang Seng (HSI) stock market index. You have some notes and a graph as your guide. Write an article of 150-200 words, making it as accurate and interesting as possible. Response: **HSI Shows Strong Recovery and Promising Outlook**

The Hang Seng Index (HSI) in Hong Kong has seen a strong and steady rise over the past six months, fueled largely by renewed investment from Mainland China. After dipping to 20,956 six months ago, the index has since climbed impressively to a recent high of 27,499 — a significant rebound that reflects growing investor confidence.

Much of this growth has been driven by the Property and Utilities sub-indices, which have surged on the back of increased activity and expansion from Chinese firms. These sectors, seen as stable and essential, have become particularly attractive in a climate of cautious optimism.

Market analysts remain bullish on the HSI’s future, predicting continued upward momentum into next year. They cite not only sustained interest from Mainland investors but also a rising wave of international capital and a recovery in Hong Kong’s domestic demand.

With both regional and global factors aligning in its favor, the HSI is positioning itself as one of Asia’s top-performing indices — offering opportunities and optimism to investors watching the post-pandemic recovery unfold.
text_b: **HSI Shows Strong Recovery and Promising Outlook** The Hang Seng Index (HSI) in Hong Kong has seen a strong and steady rise over the past six months, fueled largely by renewed investment from Mainland China. After dipping to 20,956 six months ago, the index has since climbed impressively to a recent high of 27,499 — a significant rebound that reflects growing investor confidence. Much of this growth has been driven by the Property and Utilities sub-indices, which have surged on the back of increased activity and expansion from Chinese firms. These sectors, seen as stable and essential, have become particularly attractive in a climate of cautious optimism. Market analysts remain bullish on the HSI’s future, predicting continued upward momentum into next year. They cite not only sustained interest from Mainland investors but also a rising wave of international capital and a recovery in Hong Kong’s domestic demand. With both regional and global factors aligning in its favor, the HSI is positioning itself as one of Asia’s top-performing indices — offering opportunities and optimism to investors watching the post-pandemic recovery unfold.

Prompt Level: 14.0 Prompt: Read your manager’s feedback. Redraft your speech with expressions to connect your points together and make it flow more smoothly. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: Good morning everyone,

I’d like to begin by celebrating a key milestone: we’ve successfully renewed our collaboration with one of our most valued partners. What’s more, they commended our unwavering commitment to quality—an encouraging sign that our efforts are being recognized.

That said, it’s important to reflect on the road we’ve traveled. Around three months ago, we encountered a few setbacks—specifically, some unresolved defects that impacted performance. Alongside these, we also faced a handful of technical hiccups that, while less severe, still required attention.

In response, we didn’t just patch things up—we rethought our approach. We revised how we deliver updates and strengthened our Quality Assurance practices. These improvements were only possible thanks to the outstanding collaboration across departments, which I believe is one of our greatest strengths.

Looking ahead, we’re committed to building on this momentum. We’ll continue reinforcing our support systems to prevent future issues and ensure our clients enjoy a seamless experience.

Thank you for being part of this journey.
text_b: Good morning everyone, I’d like to begin by celebrating a key milestone: we’ve successfully renewed our collaboration with one of our most valued partners. What’s more, they commended our unwavering commitment to quality—an encouraging sign that our efforts are being recognized. That said, it’s important to reflect on the road we’ve traveled. Around three months ago, we encountered a few setbacks—specifically, some unresolved defects that impacted performance. Alongside these, we also faced a handful of technical hiccups that, while less severe, still required attention. In response, we didn’t just patch things up—we rethought our approach. We revised how we deliver updates and strengthened our Quality Assurance practices. These improvements were only possible thanks to the outstanding collaboration across departments, which I believe is one of our greatest strengths. Looking ahead, we’re committed to building on this momentum. We’ll continue reinforcing our support systems to prevent future issues and ensure our clients enjoy a seamless experience. Thank you for being part of this journey.

Prompt Level: 16.0 Prompt: Write a paragraph describing how robots are being used in your country. How do you feel about them? In what ways will they positively and negatively affect society? Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: In India, robots are revolutionizing various industries, including manufacturing, healthcare, agriculture, and customer service. In manufacturing, robots perform tasks like welding, painting, and assembly, which significantly enhance productivity and efficiency. In healthcare, robots assist in surgeries, rehabilitation, and elderly care, providing precise and reliable support. Agricultural robots are used for planting, harvesting, and pesticide spraying, helping farmers increase yield and reduce labor costs. Customer service robots, such as HDFC Bank's IRA and Vistara Airlines' RADA, improve user experience by offering efficient and personalized assistance.

While robots have the potential to positively impact society by increasing efficiency, reducing human error, and performing dangerous tasks, they also pose challenges such as job displacement and the need for substantial investment in training and infrastructure. It is essential to balance technological advancement with social considerations to ensure the harmonious integration of robots into society. Additionally, ethical considerations regarding privacy and security must be addressed to prevent misuse and ensure that the benefits of robotic technology are equitably distributed across all segments of society.
text_b: In India, robots are revolutionizing various industries, including manufacturing, healthcare, agriculture, and customer service. In manufacturing, robots perform tasks like welding, painting, and assembly, which significantly enhance productivity and efficiency. In healthcare, robots assist in surgeries, rehabilitation, and elderly care, providing precise and reliable support. Agricultural robots are used for planting, harvesting, and pesticide spraying, helping farmers increase yield and reduce labor costs. Customer service robots, such as HDFC Bank's IRA and Vistara Airlines' RADA, improve user experience by offering efficient and personalized assistance. While robots have the potential to positively impact society by increasing efficiency, reducing human error, and performing dangerous tasks, they also pose challenges such as job displacement and the need for substantial investment in training and infrastructure. It is essential to balance technological advancement with social considerations to ensure the harmonious integration of robots into society. Additionally, ethical considerations regarding privacy and security must be addressed to prevent misuse and ensure that the benefits of robotic technology are equitably distributed across all segments of society.

text_a: Prompt Level: 11.0 Prompt: Write a paragraph about how media has changed in your lifetime. What media was important before? What do you use now, and how has it changed your reading habits? Pay attention to your use of commas, colons and semicolons. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Use your own words where possible. Response: Media has undergone a significant transformation in my lifetime. Previously, newspapers, magazines, and television were the primary sources of information and entertainment. People eagerly awaited the morning paper or the evening news broadcast to stay informed. However, with the advent of the internet and smartphones, digital media has taken over. Now, I primarily use online news websites, social media platforms, and streaming services for my daily dose of information and entertainment. This shift has made access to news instantaneous and more interactive. My reading habits have also changed; I now prefer reading articles and e-books on my tablet or smartphone, which allows for convenience and portability. Additionally, the rise of podcasts and audiobooks has introduced a new dimension to consuming content, making it easier to stay informed while multitasking. Overall, the evolution of media has made information more accessible and diverse, catering to a wide range of preferences and lifestyles.

text_a: Prompt Level: 11.0 Prompt: Write a paragraph about how media has changed in your lifetime. What media was important before? What do you use now, and how has it changed your reading habits? Pay attention to your use of commas, colons and semicolons. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Use your own words where possible. Response: Growing up, media was largely defined by traditional forms: newspapers delivered to the doorstep, scheduled television broadcasts, and radio programs. Before the internet's widespread adoption, information access was limited; we relied on encyclopedias and library visits for research. 1  Now, digital platforms dominate; smartphones and laptops provide instant access to global news, streaming services offer on-demand entertainment, and social media connects us with diverse perspectives. 2  My reading habits have drastically changed. I consume news through online articles and apps, often skimming headlines and summaries rather than reading in-depth articles. I listen to podcasts during commutes; I have replaced physical books with e-books and audiobooks. Social media has created echo chambers; it often shows me content that already matches my existing views. 3  The sheer volume of available information makes it challenging to distinguish credible sources from misinformation; I now prioritize fact-checking and cross-referencing information.
text_b: Growing up, media was largely defined by traditional forms: newspapers delivered to the doorstep, scheduled television broadcasts, and radio programs. Before the internet's widespread adoption, information access was limited; we relied on encyclopedias and library visits for research. 1 Now, digital platforms dominate; smartphones and laptops provide instant access to global news, streaming services offer on-demand entertainment, and social media connects us with diverse perspectives. 2 My reading habits have drastically changed. I consume news through online articles and apps, often skimming headlines and summaries rather than reading in-depth articles. I listen to podcasts during commutes; I have replaced physical books with e-books and audiobooks. Social media has created echo chambers; it often shows me content that already matches my existing views. 3 The sheer volume of available information makes it challenging to distinguish credible sources from misinformation; I now prioritize fact-checking and cross-referencing information.

Prompt Level: 16.0 Prompt: Read an email from your friend and give advice on some stressful problems he is having. Type in the input box. Write 100-200 words. Response: Hi Leon,

Thank you for opening up—I'm really sorry to hear you're going through such a tough time. It sounds like work has become a major source of stress, especially with management not recognizing your efforts. That kind of environment can really wear you down, and it’s understandable to feel drained and unmotivated. You're not alone in feeling this way, and your frustration is completely valid.

As for things with Tia, I’m really sorry that’s weighing on you too. Relationship uncertainty can feel like the ground is shifting beneath you, especially when you had hope for something more lasting. It’s okay to feel down—this is a lot to carry at once.

Give yourself credit for reaching out and being honest about how you’re feeling—that takes real strength. If you're ever unsure what to do next, maybe just start small: talk to someone you trust, write down what’s bothering you, or even think about what a better situation would look like and how you might slowly get there.

I’m here for you anytime.

Take care,
AM

Prompt Level: 13.0 Prompt: Use the information in the notes to write an informative text about NAFTA, or write about a trade agreement your country is a part of. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: The North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) was a significant trade agreement between the United States, Canada, and Mexico, which came into effect on January 1, 1994. The primary goal of NAFTA was to eliminate trade barriers and facilitate the free flow of goods and services among the three countries. By reducing tariffs and other trade restrictions, NAFTA aimed to boost economic growth and strengthen economic ties within North America.

NAFTA had several key objectives, including the reduction of trade barriers, the creation of trade rules, the improvement of working conditions, and the establishment of a safe market for North American goods and services. The agreement particularly focused on sectors such as agriculture, automotive, and textiles, gradually phasing out tariffs on these products12.

Despite its benefits, NAFTA faced criticism and controversy. Some argued that it led to job losses in certain industries due to increased competition, while others believed it contributed to economic growth and job creation overall. In 2020, NAFTA was replaced by the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA), which aimed to modernize and address the shortcomings of the original agreement12.

Overall, NAFTA played a crucial role in shaping trade relations in North America and set the stage for future trade agreements.

The primary goal of NAFTA was to eliminate trade barriers and facilitate the free flow of goods and services among the three countries. By reducing tariffs and other trade restrictions, NAFTA aimed to boost economic growth and strengthen economic ties within North America. NAFTA had several key objectives, including the reduction of trade barriers, the creation of trade rules, the improvement of working conditions, and the establishment of a safe market for North American goods and services. The agreement particularly focused on sectors such as agriculture, automotive, and textiles, gradually phasing out tariffs on these products12. Despite its benefits, NAFTA faced criticism and controversy. Some argued that it led to job losses in certain industries due to increased competition, while others believed it contributed to economic growth and job creation overall. In 2020, NAFTA was replaced by the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA), which aimed to modernize and address the shortcomings of the original agreement12. Overall, NAFTA played a crucial role in shaping trade relations in North America and set the stage for future trade agreements.

Zen is a boutique hotel that combines a spa, health, and fitness center, catering to the growing demand for wellness-focused weekend getaways. Market research from AccomData Group highlights increasing consumer interest in healthier lifestyles and dissatisfaction with the cuisine at many fitness centers. Zen will address this gap by offering a gourmet, health-conscious menu designed by top chefs from renowned restaurants. The facility will feature fully equipped gyms, swimming pools, and professional trainers, ensuring a premium wellness experience. Construction is expected to take two years, with returns on investment beginning in the second year after opening. Annual profits are projected at $1 million from the second year, with full investment recoupment anticipated within four years and sustained profitability from year five onward.

Risk Assessment
The financial projections account for potential economic downturns. However, if the economy remains stable, the investment recoupment period and profitability timeline could be significantly reduced. To ensure strong market penetration and brand recognition, an intensive media campaign will be managed by Kudos Advertising Agency, further mitigating risks and accelerating returns.

Use your own words where possible. Response: Swedish workplaces typically feature flat hierarchies, promoting open communication and idea sharing among all employees. Work-life balance is highly valued, with flexible working hours being common. Punctuality is important, and the tradition of “fika” (coffee breaks) is cherished for socializing and building relationships. Dress codes are generally casual, emphasizing comfort and practicality. Sustainability is also a priority, with many companies implementing eco-friendly practices. Understanding these aspects can help you thrive in a Swedish workplace.

text_a: Prompt Level: 7.0 Prompt: What problems can happen on board a ship? What are some solutions? Use your own ideas. Choose from one or two of the options provided and write 80-100 words. Options: general cargo safety and stowage; staffing levels and working conditions; general condition and maintenance of the vessel; safety standards; pollution Response: Problems on board a ship often stem from insufficient staffing levels and poor working conditions. Fatigue due to long hours and inadequate rest can lead to reduced efficiency and increased risk of accidents. To address this, ship operators should ensure proper staffing levels and compliance with international standards like the Maritime Labour Convention (MLC). Another common issue is inadequate maintenance of the vessel, which can lead to equipment failure and safety hazards. Regular inspections, adherence to maintenance schedules, and timely repairs can mitigate these risks, ensuring a safer and more efficient operation.

Subject: Landing Issues with Cessna 123

On [insert date of incident], a Cessna 123 encountered difficulties during landing. The primary issue was an unstable approach, leading to difficulties in achieving the correct descent rate. The pilot experienced difficulty in maintaining alignment with the runway, resulting in an overshoot during the final approach.

There were additional concerns regarding the aircraft's braking system, as the plane experienced delayed response, increasing the risk of a runway overrun. The landing gear performed as expected, but the tires showed signs of excessive wear.

After reviewing the situation, it is recommended to perform a detailed inspection of the aircraft's approach systems and braking mechanisms. Additionally, further training for pilots on handling unstable approaches could be beneficial to prevent similar incidents.

Further investigation into the cause of the unstable approach is required for a complete assessment.

Prompt Level: 13.0 Prompt: You are writing to Harry, a new manager in your company. He wants advice on how to effectively motivate his team and what strategies to avoid. Use the language and strategies you have learned in this lesson. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: Here are some ideas for motivating your employees. I also included some things to avoid.

Incentivize your employees. Make them feel that the company's success means success for them as well. There's nothing like a possible reward to motivate people.
Take an active interest in your employees' career paths. Offer them additional training and act as their mentor.
Emphasize a healthy work-life balance. Recognize that employees have lives outside the company.
Listen to your employees. If people feel like they are being heard, even when they are complaining, they will be happier and more productive.
Treat subordinates the way you would like to be treated by your superiors.

Make wellbeing a priority and also identify their priorities , so that you can easily identify what is it that they value. There is no need to push it but make your employees feel safe when having a conversation with you.

There is no need to push itbut make your employees feel safe when having a conversation with you.

Prompt Level: 11.0 Prompt: Write a paragraph about how media has changed in your lifetime. What media was important before? What do you use now, and how has it changed your reading habits? Pay attention to your use of commas, colons and semicolons. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Use your own words where possible. Response: The advent of smartphones and modern media has significantly transformed daily lifestyles. Before smartphones, communication was more deliberate and less constant; people relied on landlines or face-to-face interactions. Information was accessed through newspapers, television, or radio, often requiring more effort and time. Social interactions were more personal, with social gatherings and phone calls playing a central role. 

The smartphone era has introduced constant connectivity, enabling immediate access to information and instant communication through calls, texts, and social media. This has made it easier to stay connected with friends and family, regardless of distance. The convenience of having a camera, GPS, and internet access in one device has streamlined daily tasks and enhanced productivity. Entertainment is now more accessible, as streaming services and games are available at one's fingertips.

However, this shift also brings challenges, such as increased screen time, reduced face-to-face interactions, and potential information overload. The way people work, socialize, and consume media has become more digital, leading to a lifestyle that is simultaneously more connected and, at times, more isolated. This transformation underscores the profound impact of media and technology on modern living.

However, this shift also brings challenges, such as increased screen time, reduced face-to-face interactions, and potential information overload. The way people work, socialize, and consume media has become more digital, leading to a lifestyle that is simultaneously more connected and, at times, more isolated. This transformation underscores the profound impact of media and technology on modern living.

Prompt Level: 15.0 Prompt: Write a comparison-contrast essay about your favorite city and another place. Use the 'X then Y' structure. Be sure to polish your work before you submit it. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: Exploring the Charm of Kyoto, Then the Energy of New York City

Kyoto, Japan, is my favorite city because of its serene beauty and deep cultural roots. Walking through Kyoto feels like stepping into a living museum. Ancient temples, peaceful gardens, and traditional tea houses create a calm, reflective atmosphere. The pace of life is slower, encouraging mindfulness and appreciation of small details—like the sound of bamboo rustling or the sight of cherry blossoms falling.

Then there’s New York City, a place that pulses with energy and ambition. Skyscrapers tower over bustling streets, and every corner offers something new—street performers, food trucks, or spontaneous conversations. Unlike Kyoto’s quiet charm, New York thrives on movement and diversity. It’s a city where people chase dreams and where cultures collide in vibrant harmony.

While Kyoto offers peace and tradition, New York offers excitement and innovation. Both cities are unforgettable, but in very different ways. One soothes the soul; the other sparks the imagination.
text_b:

Kyoto, Japan, is my favorite city because of its serene beauty and deep cultural roots. Walking through Kyoto feels like stepping into a living museum. Ancient temples, peaceful gardens, and traditional tea houses create a calm, reflective atmosphere. The pace of life is slower, encouraging mindfulness and appreciation of small details—like the sound of bamboo rustling or the sight of cherry blossoms falling.

Then there’s New York City, a place that pulses with energy and ambition. Skyscrapers tower over bustling streets, and every corner offers something new—street performers, food trucks, or spontaneous conversations. Unlike Kyoto’s quiet charm, New York thrives on movement and diversity. It’s a city where people chase dreams and where cultures collide in vibrant harmony.

While Kyoto offers peace and tradition, New York offers excitement and innovation. Both cities are unforgettable, but in very different ways. One soothes the soul; the other sparks the imagination.</input>

Prompt Level: 15.0 Prompt: You're going to write about an interesting, fictional event that happened to a friend. Write your blog post. Type in the input box. Write 150-200 words. Response: Last year, I and Diana, a friend of mine who’s a Spanish teacher, were invited to participate in a voluntary Catholic mission in the US. She decided to take the invite and quit her job, but I declined it. It takes a lot of courage and faith to leave Brazil with little money and lots of fears. 
As she arrived there, she  was very well received by the church community. She got a nice room with such friendly roommates. Little did she know of the ventures she’d face, though.
The first hindrance was communication. She was fluent in Spanish but had poor English. But she got some useful survival lessons from her roommates. Another issue that made Diana rethink her decision to leave Brazil was the weather – she arrived in New York by Thanksgiving. Can you imagine how cold she felt? Once again, her mates helped her by sharing their winter clothes and buying her new ones. They even managed to find Diana a part-time job at a language school, so she could have her own money while staying there. Had I known she’d meet such supporting people, I’d have gone with her.

She decided to take the invite and quit her job, but I declined it. It takes a lot of courage and faith to leave Brazil with little money and lots of fears. As she arrived there, she was very well received by the church community. She got a nice room with such friendly roommates. Little did she know of the ventures she’d face, though. The first hindrance was communication. She was fluent in Spanish but had poor English. But she got some useful survival lessons from her roommates. Another issue that made Diana rethink her decision to leave Brazil was the weather – she arrived in New York by Thanksgiving. Can you imagine how cold she felt? Once again, her mates helped her by sharing their winter clothes and buying her new ones. They even managed to find Diana a part-time job at a language school, so she could have her own money while staying there. Had I known she’d meet such supporting people, I’d have gone with her.
